word_list.word: revenge is a dish best served cold
word_list.pronunciation: ɹɪvˈɛndʒ ɪz ɐ dˈɪʃ bˈɛst sˈɜːvd kˈoʊld

senses.definition: used to suggest that it is more effective to wait and plan carefully before seeking revenge, rather than acting impulsively or emotionally

examples.x: When my former business partner betrayed me, I knew that revenge is a dish best served cold, so I bided my time and waited until the right moment to strike.

word_list.word: an eye for an eye makes the whole world blind
word_list.pronunciation: ɐn ˈaɪ fɚɹən ˈaɪ mˌeɪks ðə hˈoʊl wˈɜːld blˈaɪnd

senses.definition: used to imply that seeking revenge or retribution for a wrongdoing only leads to a never-ending cycle of retaliation and violence, ultimately causing harm to everyone involved

examples.x: The survivor of a violent attack forgave their attacker, stating that an eye for an eye makes the whole world blind and choosing to break the cycle of violence rather than seek revenge.

senses.definition: used to caution against violent or vengeful actions, as it suggests that such actions can lead to a cycle of violence and revenge that is difficult to break

examples.x: The detective warned the gang members that blood will have blood, and that their violent actions would only lead to more violence and retribution.

word_list.word: to forget (a|) wrong, is the best revenge
word_list.pronunciation: tə fɚɡˈɛt ɐ ɔːɹ ɹˈɔŋ ɪz ðə bˈɛst ɹɪvˈɛndʒ

senses.definition: used to imply that the most effective way to retaliate against someone who has wronged one is to let go of the anger and move on with one's life, rather than seeking revenge or holding a grudge

examples.x: I used to hold a grudge against my former boss for passing me over for a promotion, but then I realized that to forget wrong is the best revenge, and I focused on building my own successful career instead.

word_list.pronunciation: tˈuː blˈæks nˌɑːt mˌeɪk ɐ wˈaɪt

senses.definition: used to imply that it is not justifiable to respond to a bad act with another bad act, and that two wrongs do not make a right

examples.x: Just because you were mistreated doesn't give you the right to mistreat someone else - remember, two blacks never make a white.

word_list.pronunciation: tˈuː ɹˈɔŋz nˌɑːt mˌeɪk ɐ ɹˈaɪt

senses.definition: used to imply that responding to a wrong or injustice with another wrong or injustice does not make the situation better or right

examples.x: When you're tempted to seek revenge, remember that two wrongs never make a right and that the best response to bad behavior is to act in a manner that is just and moral.

senses.definition: used to imply that in response to being wronged, one should seek justice or revenge in a calm and calculated manner, rather than reacting with anger or frustration
used to suggest that it is acceptable to retaliate against someone who has wronged one in the same manner

examples.x: She was always taking credit for my work, so I figured turnabout is fair play and started doing the same to her.

senses.definition: used to imply that there is a sense of satisfaction or pleasure in getting even with someone who has wronged one

examples.x: When I finally got revenge on my ex for cheating on me, it felt like revenge is sweet, but I also realized that it didn't make me feel any better in the long run.
